extraction.one_line_summary: VIVIANI Elia won stage 3 sprint at Teramo by jumping with 500m to go when peloton hesitated, beating SAGAN Peter and GAVIRIA Fernando
tactical_patterns: VIVIANI Elia attacked at 500m when peloton decelerated post-corner, exploiting hesitation rather than waiting for leadout positioning
Quick-Step lost leadout cohesion in technical finale forcing VIVIANI solo acceleration, suggesting corners negate team sprint trains
SAGAN Peter positioned himself wheel-hunting without leadout in top-10 at 1km despite physical struggles, veteran positioning skill compensates
Breakaway absorbed extremely late suggesting bunch feared losing time on technical finish more than contesting intermediate points

rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: positive: executed 500m solo sprint after losing leadout, shows he can win without perfect positioning on long stages
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: finished second despite being visibly underweight and having physical troubles, losing peak sprint power
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: neutral: podium finish but no tactical initiative shown, reactive rather than proactive in chaotic finale
rider_signals.SCHÖNBERGER Sebastian: neutral: launched three separate attacks including intermediate sprint contest, aggressive but ultimately ineffective breakaway attempts
rider_signals.TONELLI Alessandro: neutral: featured in late breakaway attempts absorbed close to finish
